question: According to the World Health Organisation (WHO), reproductive health means a total well-being in all aspects of reproduction, i.e., physical, emotional, behavioural and social.

India was amongst the first countries in the world to initiate action plans and programmes at a national level to attain total reproductive health as a social goal. These programmes called ‘family planning’ were initiated in
Improved programmes covering wider reproduction-related areas are currently in operation under the popular name
options: Reproductive and Child Health Care (RCH) programmes

question: Introduction of sex education in schools should also be encouraged to provide right information to the young so as to discourage children from believing in myths and having misconceptions about sex-related aspects.
options: Should be optional only for girls

question: Proper information about reproductive organs, adolescence and related changes, safe and hygienic sexual practices, sexually transmitted diseases (STD), AIDS, etc., would help people, especially those in the adolescent age group to lead a reproductively healthy life.
options: Especially those in the adolescent age group

question: Statutory ban on amniocentesis for sex-determination to legally check increasing menace of female foeticides.
options: Is a programme that merits mention

question: In amniocentesis some of the amniotic fluid of the developing foetus is taken to analyse the fetal cells and dissolved substances. This procedure is used to test for the presence of certain genetic disorders such as, down syndrome, haemophilia, sickle-cell anemia, etc.
options: Genetic disorders such as down syndrome, haemophilia, sickle-cell anemia

question: 'Saheli'-a new oral contraceptive for the females-was developed by scientists at

Creating awareness among people about various reproduction related aspects and providing facilities and support for building up a reproductively healthy society are the major tasks under these programmes.

Educating people, especially fertile couples and those in marriageable age group, about available birth control options, care of pregnant mothers, post-natal care of the mother and child, importance of breast feeding, equal opportunities for the male and the female child, etc., would address the importance of bringing up socially conscious healthy families of desired size.
options: Bringing up socially conscious healthy families of desired size

question: Biotechnology, as you would have learnt from the previous chapter, essentially deals with industrial scale production of biopharmaceuticals and biologicals using genetically modified microbes, fungi, plants and animals.
options: Genetically modified microbes, fungi, plants and animals

question: The applications of biotechnology include therapeutics, diagnostics, genetically modified crops for agriculture, processed food, bioremediation, waste treatment, and energy production.
options: Therapeutics, diagnostics, genetically modified crops, processed food, bioremediation, waste treatment, and energy production

question: Three critical research areas of biotechnology are: (i) Providing the best catalyst in the form of improved organism usually a microbe or pure enzyme. (ii) Creating optimal conditions through engineering for a catalyst to act, and (iii) Downstream processing technologies to purify the protein/organic compound.

Biotechnological applications in agriculture include three options for increasing food production: (i) agro-chemical based agriculture; (ii) organic agriculture; and (iii) genetically engineered crop-based agriculture.
The Green Revolution succeeded in tripling the food supply but yet it was not enough to feed the growing human population.
Tissue culture is a method where whole plants could be regenerated from explants, i.e., any part of a plant taken out and grown in a test tube, under sterile conditions in special nutrient media.
The capacity to generate a whole plant from any cell/explant is called totipotency.
This method of producing thousands of plants through tissue culture is called micro-propagation.
Each of these plants will be genetically identical to the original plant from which they were grown, i.e., they are somaclones.
Many important food plants like tomato, banana, apple, etc., have been produced on commercial scale using this method.
An ecosystem can be visualised as a functional unit of nature, where living organisms interact among themselves and also with the surrounding physical environment.
options: Living organisms interact among themselves and also with the surrounding physical environment

question: Ecosystem varies greatly in size from a small pond to a large forest or a sea.
options: From a small pond to a large forest or a sea

question: Many ecologists regard the entire biosphere as a global ecosystem, as a composite of all local ecosystems on Earth.
options: The entire biosphere as a global ecosystem

question: Forest, grassland and desert are some examples of terrestrial ecosystems.
Pond, lake, wetland, river and estuary are some examples of aquatic ecosystems.
Crop fields and an aquarium may also be considered as man-made ecosystems.
The components of the ecosystem are seen to function as a unit when you consider the following aspects: (i) Productivity; (ii) Decomposition; (iii) Energy flow; and (iv) Nutrient cycling.
To understand the ethos of an aquatic ecosystem let us take a small pond as an example.
A pond is a shallow water body in which all the above mentioned four basic components of an ecosystem are well exhibited.
The abiotic component is the water with all the dissolved inorganic and organic substances and the rich soil deposit at the bottom of the pond.
options: Water with dissolved substances and soil deposit

question: Biodiversity is the term popularised by the sociobiologist Edward Wilson to describe the combined diversity at all the levels of biological organisation.
options: Combined diversity at all the levels of biological organisation

question: Genetic diversity: A single species might show high diversity at the genetic level over its distributional range.
options: High diversity at the genetic level

question: The genetic variation shown by the medicinal plant Rauwolfia vomitoria growing in different Himalayan ranges might be in terms of the potency and concentration of the active chemical (reserpine) that the plant produces.
India has more than 50,000 genetically different strains of rice, and 1,000 varieties of mango.
options: More than 50,000 strains of rice and 1,000 varieties of mango

question: Species diversity: The diversity at the species level, for example, the Western Ghats have a greater amphibian species diversity than the Eastern Ghats.
options: Western Ghats have a greater amphibian species diversity

question: Ecological diversity: At the ecosystem level, India, for instance, with its deserts, rain forests, mangroves, coral reefs, wetlands, estuaries, and alpine meadows has a greater ecosystem diversity than a Scandinavian country like Norway.
It has taken millions of years of evolution, to accumulate this rich diversity in nature, but we could lose all that wealth in less than two centuries if the present rates of species losses continue.

Biodiversity and its conservation are now vital environmental issues of international concern as more and more people around the world begin to realise the critical importance of biodiversity for our survival and well-being on this planet.
options: Vital environmental issues of international concern

question: According to the International Union for Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources (IUCN) (2004), the total number of plant and animal species described so far is slightly more than 1.5 million.
A more conservative and scientifically sound estimate made by Robert May places the global species diversity at about 7 million.
DNA acts as the genetic material in most of the organisms.
RNA though it also acts as a genetic material in some viruses, mostly functions as a messenger.
options: In some viruses as genetic material, mostly as messenger

question: DNA is a long polymer of deoxyribonucleotides.
A bacteriophage known as ϕ×174 has 5386 nucleotides.
Escherichia coli has 4.6 × 10^6 bp.
Haploid content of human DNA is 3.3 × 10^9 bp.
A nucleotide has three components – a nitrogenous base, a pentose sugar (ribose in case of RNA, and deoxyribose for DNA), and a phosphate group.
options: Nitrogenous base, pentose sugar, phosphate group

question: There are two types of nitrogenous bases – Purines (Adenine and Guanine), and Pyrimidines (Cytosine, Uracil and Thymine).
Cytosine is common for both DNA and RNA and Thymine is present in DNA. Uracil is present in RNA at the place of Thymine.
options: Cytosine common, Thymine in DNA, Uracil in RNA

question: A nitrogenous base is linked to the OH of 1' C pentose sugar through a N-glycosidic linkage to form a nucleoside.
Microbes are present everywhere – in soil, water, air, inside our bodies and that of other animals and plants.
options: Everywhere including soil, water, air, bodies

question: They are present even at sites where no other life-form could possibly exist–sites such as deep inside the geysers (thermal vents) where the temperature may be as high as 100°C.
Microbes are diverse–protozoa, bacteria, fungi and microscopic animal and plant viruses, viroids and also prions that are proteinacious infectious agents.
options: Protozoa, bacteria, fungi, viruses, viroids, prions

question: Microbes like bacteria and many fungi can be grown on nutritive media to form colonies, that can be seen with the naked eyes.
options: Can be seen with naked eyes

question: Such cultures are useful in studies on micro-organisms.
The Big Bang theory attempts to explain to us the origin of universe.
The universe is very old – almost 13.8 billion years old.
Earth was supposed to have been formed about 4.5 billion years back.
Life appeared 500 million years after the formation of earth, i.e., almost four billion years back.

Oparin of Russia and Haldane of England proposed that the first form of life could have come from pre-existing non-living organic molecules (e.g. RNA, protein, etc.) and that formation of life was preceded by chemical evolution.
options: Chemical evolution from pre-existing non-living organic molecules

question: The male reproductive system is located in the pelvis region.
The testes are situated outside the abdominal cavity within a pouch called scrotum.
The scrotum helps in maintaining the low temperature of the testes (2–2.5° C lower than the normal internal body temperature) necessary for spermatogenesis.
Each testis has about 250 compartments called testicular lobules.
Each lobule contains one to three highly coiled seminiferous tubules in which sperms are produced.
The male germ cells undergo meiotic divisions finally leading to sperm formation, while Sertoli cells provide nutrition to the germ cells.
options: Meiotic divisions for germ cells, nutrition by Sertoli

question: Leydig cells synthesise and secrete testicular hormones called androgens.
The male sex accessory ducts include rete testis, vasa efferentia, epididymis and vas deferens.
options: Rete testis, vasa efferentia, epididymis and vas deferens

question: The seminiferous tubules of the testis open into the vasa efferentia through rete testis.
options: Through rete testis to vasa efferentia

question: The epididymis leads to vas deferens that ascends to the abdomen and loops over the urinary bladder.
options: Ascends to abdomen and loops over urinary bladder

question: Microbes in household products include curd, idli, dosa, etc., produced by bacteria and yeast.
Microbes in industrial products include antibiotics, enzymes, etc., produced by microbes.
Microbes in sewage treatment help in reducing BOD.
Methanogens in biogas production produce methane.
Microbes as biocontrol agents include Bacillus thuringiensis for pest control.
Microbes as biofertilisers include Rhizobium, Azotobacter, etc.
The first non-cellular forms of life could have originated 3 billion years back.
They would have been giant molecules (RNA, Protein, Polysaccharides, etc.).
The Oparin-Haldane theory is also known as chemical evolution theory.
S.L. Miller created similar conditions in laboratory and observed formation of amino acids.
Genetically engineered crop-based agriculture is one option for increasing food production.
Tissue culture got developed as traditional breeding techniques failed to keep pace with demand.
The nutrient medium must provide a carbon source such as sucrose and also inorganic salts, vitamins, amino acids and growth regulators like auxins, cytokinins etc.
options: Carbon source like sucrose, salts, vitamins, amino acids, auxins, cytokinins

question: Recovery of healthy plants from diseased plants is possible by culturing meristems.
Isolated protoplasts from two different varieties of plants can be fused to get hybrid protoplasts, which can be further grown to form a new plant. These hybrids are called somatic hybrids.
The solar input, the cycle of temperature, day-length and other climatic conditions regulate the rate of function of the entire pond.
options: Solar input, temperature cycle, day-length, climatic conditions

question: The autotrophic components include the phytoplankton, some algae and the floating, submerged and marginal plants found at the edges.
options: Phytoplankton, algae, floating, submerged and marginal plants

question: The consumers are represented by the zooplankton, the free swimming and bottom dwelling forms.
options: Zooplankton, free swimming and bottom dwelling forms

question: The decomposers are the fungi, bacteria and flagellates especially abundant in the bottom of the pond.
There is unidirectional movement of energy towards the higher trophic levels and its dissipation and loss as heat to the environment.
The Western Ghats have a greater amphibian species diversity than the Eastern Ghats.
India, for instance, with its deserts, rain forests, mangroves, coral reefs, wetlands, estuaries, and alpine meadows has a greater ecosystem diversity than a Scandinavian country like Norway.
Biodiversity and its conservation are now vital environmental issues of international concern.
Some extreme estimates range from 20 to 50 million, but a more conservative estimate by Robert May places global species diversity at about 7 million.
A nitrogenous base is linked to the OH of 1' C pentose sugar through a N-glycosidic linkage to form a nucleoside.
Two nucleotides are linked through 3'-5' phosphodiester linkage to form a dinucleotide.
DNA has a free phosphate moiety at 5' end and a free 3' OH at the other end.
The backbone of a polynucleotide chain is formed by sugar and phosphate.
The nitrogenous bases are linked to sugar moiety by N-glycosidic linkage.
The two chains of DNA have anti-parallel polarity.
